flow.no: Proceed to check the condensate pump.
flow.yes: Clear the drain line and monitor for leaks.
flow.title: Check the Drain Line
actions: Inspect the drain line for clogs
Look for water pooling around the unit
flow.field_insight: Use a wet/dry vacuum to clear stubborn clogs.
flow.interpretation: A clogged drain line can cause water to back up and leak.

flow.title: Check the Condensate Pump
actions: Inspect the pump for signs of damage
Listen for unusual noises from the pump
flow.field_insight: Ensure the pump is plugged in and receiving power.
flow.interpretation: A faulty condensate pump can fail to remove water, leading to leaks.

flow.title: Check the Air Filter
actions: Remove and inspect the air filter
flow.field_insight: Regularly changing your air filter can prevent many HVAC issues.
flow.interpretation: A dirty air filter can restrict airflow, causing condensation to build up.

flow.no: Monitor the system for further issues.
flow.title: Check the Ductwork
actions: Look for signs of moisture around ducts
flow.field_insight: Seal any leaks with duct tape or mastic sealant.
flow.interpretation: Damaged ductwork can lead to leaks and inefficient system performance.
